consistency_overall.summary: The trajectory moderately matches a socially oriented, accommodating extrovert, but it is more disciplined and task-managed than the persona’s weakness profile would suggest.

swap_detail.justification: Trajectory strongly matches Persona A’s high academic priority, short-horizon deadline chasing, low sleep discipline, avoidant-but-direct async messaging, and repeated work/sleep oscillation under fatigue; B would be more balanced and compromise-oriented.

The trajectory strongly matches Persona A’s heavy academic priority, low sleep discipline, short-horizon deadline-chasing, and avoidant/async teammate message; the repeated low-energy, burnout-avoiding choices and willingness to trade sleep for deadlines are much less consistent with Persona B.

Trajectory emphasizes academic prioritization, medium-horizon deadline management, text-based teammate messaging, and compromise under low energy—closer to Persona A’s balanced, compromising style than Persona B’s relationship-first, accommodating, face-to-face social focus.

Trajectory emphasizes medium-horizon deadline management, compromise with teammates via text, and balancing work with sleep recovery—matching Persona A’s balanced priorities, text preference, and compromising conflict style more than Persona B’s relationship-heavy, face-to-face, accommodating pattern.

The trajectory repeatedly prioritizes hard deadlines over relationships, treats the team meeting as a soft cost, and uses sleep only as a tactical recovery move after pushing work—matching Persona B’s academic-heavy, short-horizon, sleep-deprived profile more than Persona A’s balanced, compromise-oriented baseline.

The trajectory shows academic-over-social prioritization, avoidant-but-direct async messaging, high risk tolerance, short planning horizon, procrastination/sleep debt patterns, and repeatedly treating the team meeting as a soft cost—matching Persona A’s values and decision tendencies much more than Persona B’s balanced/compromising style.

Trajectory is strongly achievement/deadline focused with repeated prioritization of hard academic tasks over the team meeting, plus explicit ‘sunk/irreversible loss’ language and limited relationship concern, matching Persona B’s high academic weighting and loss-framing.

The trajectory shows academic-first prioritization, avoidant but direct async messaging, and repeated misclassification of a soft team meeting as low-cost compared with hard deadlines—matching Persona A’s heavy academic weighting, avoidant conflict style, and tendency to deprioritize relationships.

Trajectory is relationship-weighted and accommodating: it repeatedly prioritizes teammate trust, direct team communication, and short-horizon deadline juggling, matching Persona A’s high relationship priority, short planning horizon, and social-over-self tendencies better than Persona B’s more academic-balanced, compromising/text-oriented baseline.

Trajectory shows compromise-oriented prioritization of academics with some relationship maintenance, text-based teammate reassurance, and explicit concern about not ghosting teammate_A, matching Persona A's balanced priorities and compromising style more than Persona B's more social/accommodating profile.

The trajectory strongly matches Persona A’s academic-first priorities, sleep-debt/energy management, avoidant but direct async messaging, and tendency to preserve teammate trust while deprioritizing relationships; Persona B would be less likely to frame decisions around academic deadlines and low-energy recovery.

Trajectory strongly matches Tired High-Achiever: academic priority dominates, avoidant async teammate messaging, repeated sleep to manage debt/stress, and willingness to deprioritize social/health in favor of deadline-driven task switching.
